decisions.reasoning: The game just started, and both players have no pieces on the board yet. Building a settlement is the first priority. The BRICK 2:1 port at node 52 is a good option as it allows for efficient trading of BRICKs. Node 52 also has BRICK production from 5 (4 pips), 6 (5 pips), and 10 (3 pips), providing a steady resource stream. No other nodes offer as good a combination of production and port.

As the first player, I should secure key resources and strategic positions. Building a road from my settlement on node 52 to node 53 is crucial to connect my settlement and secure the BRICK 2:1 port at node 53. This move also helps in future expansion.

As the first player, I should aim to secure a settlement early to gain an initial advantage. The BRICK 2:1 port at node 52 is a strong option, as BRICK is a versatile resource and the port allows for efficient trading. The tile produces BRICK on 5 (4 pips), 6 (5 pips), and 10 (3 pips), giving me a good chance to collect BRICKs. The robber is on a BRICK-producing tile, so securing a settlement here will prevent the opponent from stealing BRICKs while also gaining the port.

As the first player, I should expand quickly to gain resources and settlements. Building a road from my settlement@16 to an open intersection is the best immediate move to secure resources and prepare for future settlements.

As the first player, I should focus on expanding and securing resources. The most versatile and valuable resource is wheat, as it appears on multiple tiles and has a port for easy trading. Building a settlement at node 28 would give me access to wheat from two tiles and a port for easy trading, which is a strong strategic move.
I need to build a road to connect to my second settlement. The road between (28, 29) is the only option available.

Blue needs to secure more resources and establish a strong position. Building a settlement on node 28 (SHEEP on 10, WOOD on 9, WHEAT on 3) is a good option as it covers multiple resources and is close to an opponent's settlement, providing a strategic advantage. It also sits on a 3:1 port, which is valuable for trading.

Blue needs to expand and gain resources. Building a road from 9 to 10 is the best option as it connects to a settlement and allows access to the BRICK port at node 52, which is currently unoccupied by the opponent.

The robber must be moved to a tile that produces resources for me or stops a tile producing for my opponent. The BRICK and ORE tiles are good targets. Moving to BRICK 5 or ORE 8 would be ideal, but the robber is already on BRICK 5, so moving to ORE 8 is the best option.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the opponent has no brick cards. Trading wheat for brick at a 2:1 rate is beneficial. The opponent has 4 wheat cards, so this trade will maximize resource gain.

The opponent has a strong hand with 2 brick and 2 ore, which could be used to build cities. To prevent the opponent from gaining an advantage, I should build a settlement to block their road expansion. The best spot is node 25, which is near the opponent's settlement and has a sheep port for a 2:1 trade.

The opponent has more ore, so trading ore for other resources is beneficial. The 3:1 port for brick and the 2:1 ports for wheat and wood offer good trade rates. Trading ore for brick is the best option as it maximizes resource diversity and value.

The opponent has two sheep, which means they can play a Monopoly card to take all of my sheep. I need to act to prevent this. Building a settlement on node 35 would give me a sheep-producing tile and help me avoid losing all my sheep. The 2:1 sheep port at node 47 is close, but I need to build first. There are no immediate threats or opportunities to gain VP, so building is the best move.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the opponent has brick resources. Trading for brick is not beneficial. The opponent has sheep and can monopolize it, so trading for sheep is risky. Trading for ore or wood is not optimal given the opponent's resources. Trading for wheat is the best option as it is a common resource and the opponent has only one wheat.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have 2 wheat in hand. Trading 2 wheat for 1 brick is a good use of resources, as brick is needed for roads and settlements. No other resource is as valuable to me at this moment.

The opponent has more wheat and I need resources to build. Trading 2 wheat for 1 brick is beneficial as it allows me to build a road or settlement. The opponent has 3 wheat, so they will not be able to trade back effectively.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the opponent has more brick resources. Trading for wheat or ore would be beneficial. Trading for sheep or wood is less appealing due to the opponent's holdings. Trading for wheat gives a better rate (3:1) and is more valuable.
I need to increase my resource production. Building a city on node 28 would produce 2 wheat per turn, which is more valuable than other options. The 2:1 wheat port further enhances this.

The robber is currently on a wheat-producing tile (tile -2, 0, 2), and moving it would prevent the opponent from producing wheat. The opponent has 3 victory points, so I need to take actions that will either increase my score or hinder the opponent. Moving the robber to a wheat-producing tile is the most direct way to interfere with the opponent's resource collection.

The opponent has a city at 28, which produces wheat on 3, 5, 8, and 12. Since I have no wheat in my hand and the dice are likely to roll 3, 5, or 8, it's beneficial to trade for wheat. The 2:1 wheat-port at node 28 can be used to trade 3 wheat for 1 wood, which is a good deal.

The opponent has a city at 28, which produces wheat on 3 and 8. I need resources to build more settlements and cities. Trading wheat for brick or ore would help build roads, but I have enough ore. Trading wheat for sheep or wood would help build settlements, but I have enough sheep. Trading wheat for brick is the best option as it helps build roads and supports future settlements and cities.

I need to build a settlement to increase my resource production. The available options are to trade for brick, wheat, ore, or wood. Trading for brick is the best option as it allows me to build a settlement on the node 52, which is already connected to one of my roads and is two edges away from any other settlements or cities.

The robber must be moved to a productive tile to maximize resource collection. The tile (2, -1, -1) produces ORE (5 pips), WOOD (9 pips), and WHEAT (8 pips). Moving the robber there will prevent production from these tiles and allow stealing from the opponent if they have a settlement or city there. The opponent has a city at 28, which is connected to the tile (2, -1, -1), so moving the robber there will steal from the opponent.

The opponent is at 2 VP and needs to be outplayed. I have a city at 28 producing ORE and WHEAT. Building a city at 16 would not provide any new resources but would give 2 VP. Trading at the port for brick or ore is not beneficial as I already have enough resources. Trading wheat for wood is not optimal either. The best action is to build a city at 16 to gain 2 VP.
End turn as there are no immediate opportunities to gain resources or build.

The opponent has a city at 28, which produces wheat. I can trade wheat for wood at a 2:1 rate at the port. This is a good deal since I have 4 wheat and 3 brick, and I need wood for future builds. Trading 2 wheat for 1 wood is better than the 4:1 rate.

The opponent has 2 VP and 4 wheat, and is close to a victory. I need to increase my VP and resources. Building a city on 28 would give me 2 VP and more resource production, but I only have 2 roads left. Building a settlement on 29 would allow me to build another road and get more resources, so I'll choose that.
End turn since there are no immediate building opportunities that would significantly increase VP or resources. The opponent has more VP and resources, so playing defensively is key.

The opponent has a significant lead in VP and resources. The only action available is maritime trade, and since the hand has 5 wheat, trading 4 wheat for 1 wood is the best option to increase resource diversity and potentially build more settlements or roads.
Need to reduce hand size to 7. ORE is the most valuable resource to discard as it is not a port and I have the most of it. Discarding ORE will also prevent the opponent from monopolizing it.
Need to reduce hand size due to the 7 roll rule; brick is most abundant and discarding it does not impact future actions.
Need to reduce hand size due to 7 roll. ORE is most abundant in hand and brick is least, so discard ORE to maintain resource balance.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have excess brick cards. Discarding a brick card will reduce the chance of a 7 being rolled and stealing from an opponent, which is currently beneficial as the opponent has no resources.

The opponent has a city on a wheat-producing tile and no cards, making them vulnerable to resource theft. I should play a knight card to steal from them. I have enough resources to play a knight card.

I need to reduce my hand size due to the 7 roll. I have 4 wheat, which is the most and can be used for multiple actions. Discarding a brick is the best option as it's the only resource I have multiple of and it's not needed for immediate actions.

The robber is currently on (2, 0, -2), which is a WOOD-producing tile. Moving the robber to a tile that produces more resources for me is key. The best options are moving to a WHEAT-producing tile, as it has a 5-pip count. Moving to (1, -1, 0) would place the robber on a WHEAT-producing tile at node 28, which is a good strategic choice.
Need to gain resources and expand. Can't build a settlement or city with current hand. Trading for wood is the best option given the ports and current resource counts.

I need to build a road to connect to my city at node 28 and expand my network. Building a road to node 29 would allow me to connect to the wheat-producing tile and potentially a future settlement. The other options either involve trading or buying a development card, which are less immediate in terms of resource acquisition.

The robber should be moved to block the opponent's ore production, as they have a settlement on the ore tile (52) and no development cards to counter. The ore tile produces on 4, 8, and 11, which are relatively rare rolls. Moving the robber to (0, -1, 1) will block the ore tile for the opponent.

The opponent has no resources and is behind in VP. Building a settlement or road is most beneficial. Building a settlement at node 9 would give access to brick and ore production, which are scarce. Building a road at (5, 16) would connect to the city and potentially claim the longest road bonus.

I need resources to build and expand. I have excess wheat and ore, and I can use them to get wood and brick through the ports. The WHEAT 2:1 port at node 28 is the most efficient for getting wood, and the ORE 2:1 port at node 25 is the most efficient for getting brick. I also need to consider the robber position, but currently it's not on a productive tile for me.

I need to build a road to expand my network and potentially block the opponent's longest road. The best options are BUILD_ROAD (10, 29) or BUILD_ROAD (29, 30). BUILD_ROAD (10, 29) connects to my city at 28 and prevents the opponent from expanding their longest road.

End turn is not optimal as I can still build or buy a card. Maritime trades are not beneficial given my hand. Building a road is the best option as it allows me to expand and potentially get resources from a port.
The opponent is behind in VP and pieces. I should buy a development card to gain an advantage. The Year of Plenty card is particularly useful for gaining resources.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so rolling a 5 or 6 will stop production. Playing a knight now would be beneficial as it can move the robber and potentially steal resources. No other action is as immediately advantageous.

The robber is currently on (0, -1, 1), which is a brick-producing tile. Moving the robber away from this tile is key to preventing the opponent from stealing resources. The opponent has a settlement at (52, 53), so moving the robber to (1, -1, 0) will prevent the opponent from stealing resources from this tile. This is the most strategic move given the current situation.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so I should move it to block a tile that would produce for my opponent. The most valuable tile to block is the wheat-producing one at (28, 29), as it produces on 3, 3, 5, 8, and 11, which are frequent rolls. Moving the robber there will stop my opponent's city from collecting resources. I don't have enough resources to build a settlement or road, and I don't have any development cards to play. Trading isn't beneficial as I don't have enough of any resource to make a good trade.

I need to build roads to expand my network and secure resource production. The BRICK port at node 52 is a good option for gaining resources. Building a road to node 52 would allow me to access the BRICK port and secure the BRICK-producing tile at node 52.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile, which is not very valuable. Moving the robber to a WHEAT-producing tile would be more beneficial. The tile at (-1, 1, 0) produces WHEAT on 3 and 8, and is not occupied by the opponent. Moving the robber there will also allow me to steal from the opponent if they have WHEAT.

The opponent has a city and is close to 10 VP, while I have a city but no development cards. The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so trading for brick might be useful. The 4:1 trade is better than the 3:1 port trade for brick.

The robber is currently on a brick-producing tile, and moving it to a wheat-producing tile would be beneficial. Blue has a city on a wheat-producing tile (node 28), so moving the robber there would steal a wheat card from Blue. Red needs resources to build and has a shortage of wood and brick, which are not produced by any of the current settlements or cities. Moving the robber to a wheat-producing tile will also prevent Blue from producing wheat this turn.
Blue needs to gain resources and VP. The opponent has a longer road and more VP. Blue has 4 wheat and needs to trade to gain resources. The 2:1 wheat-port trade is the best option.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have 3 brick cards. Trading wheat for brick at the 2:1 rate is a good use of resources. My opponent has no brick or ore, so they won't be able to take advantage of the 2:1 trade. This action also helps me manage my resources.

The opponent is behind in VP and has no development cards, so they are unlikely to play a knight. The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have 4 brick cards, which is a good ratio for a 4:1 trade. Trading bricks for wheat or ore would be less beneficial, and I don't have any sheep or wood to trade.

The opponent is behind in VP and pieces, so they are unlikely to be a threat. I have a city and a large longest road, but need more VP. I have enough resources to build a settlement, which will give me 1 VP and more options for future growth. The BRICK and ORE ports are not useful given my hand, but the WHEAT port is. I can build a settlement on node 32, which is a WHEAT port and will produce 1 wheat on a roll of 3 or 8, both of which have high pips.

The opponent has no development cards and only 2 VP, so they are likely focused on resource collection. The robber is on a BRICK-producing tile, which means BRICKs will not be produced this turn. My longest road is 3, so I need to extend it to 5 to get the 2 VP for the longest road. I have 11 roads left, so building a road is affordable. Building a settlement or city is not as urgent since I have no development cards to play and the robber is on a BRICK-producing tile. Trading for BRICKs is not beneficial since they are not produced. Trading for WHEAT, WOOD, SHEEP, or ORE is also not beneficial since they are not produced either. Therefore, building a road is the best option.

Need to build a settlement or road to gain resources or VP. The opponent is at 2 VP and is close to 10, so building a settlement or road is crucial. The BRICK port at node 52 is a good option as it produces BRICK on 5, 6, and 10. Building a settlement on node 52 would give access to BRICK resources and is within the distance rule.

I need to expand my road network and potentially build a settlement or city. Building a road is the most immediate action. Road (2, 3) connects to my existing road and settlement, and does not block any future moves.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so a roll of 5, 6, or 10 would produce. The opponent has a settlement on a brick-producing tile (52), so I should consider stealing from them. The opponent has 1 brick, so I need to trade wheat for brick. The 2:1 wheat-to-brick port at node 53 is the best option.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have excess ore. Trading ore for brick is beneficial as it increases my brick supply, which is currently lacking. I have no immediate need for wheat, wood, or sheep, and playing a development card or building would require resources I can't afford with my current hand.

The opponent has a significant lead in VP and longest road. My hand is weak in resources, and I need to build roads to expand and potentially get to more resources. Building a road from my settlement at 52 to the port at 53 would allow me to use the 2:1 brick port, which is crucial for my limited resources. Additionally, it helps me expand and could lead to more strategic placements in the future.

The opponent has a city at 28 and a settlement at 52, and I have cities at 16 and 28. I need to expand my road network and potentially build a settlement or city. The opponent has a settlement at 52 which is close to my cities, so I should build a road to connect to my city at 16. This move helps secure my longest road and prevents the opponent from cutting off my road network.

Red needs to build to increase VP and resources. The opponent has 2 VP and Red has 4, so Red needs to build a city or settlement to close the gap. Red has enough ore to build a city at node 28, which would give 2 VP and help secure the longest road. Red also has the resources to build a road, but building a city is more strategic given the current VP difference.

The opponent has a city and is closer to 10 VP. The only way to gain VP is to build a city or get more resources. The opponent has a settlement and road near my wheat-producing tiles, so I should block them with a settlement. The 2:1 wheat port at node 28 is a good option.

The opponent has 2 VP and 3 settlements, but only 2 cities left to build. I have 4 VP and 2 cities, but only 5 settlements left. The robber is on a brick tile, which is producing for the opponent. I should move the robber to a tile that produces for me and steal from the opponent. The WHEAT port is the best option as it offers a 3:1 trade, and I have 2 wheat in my hand.

Need to build roads to expand and potentially block the opponent. The best options are to build roads that connect to existing settlements or cities. Building road (2, 9) connects to the city at node 16, which is a strategic move. Building road (29, 30) connects to the city at node 28, which is also strategic. However, building road (2, 9) is slightly better as it directly connects to the city and expands the road network.

The opponent has a city and is closer to 10 VP. I have a city and need resources to build more settlements and potentially a road to expand. Trading ore for brick, wheat, wood, or sheep would not significantly help me reach 10 VP. Ending my turn is the best option as I do not have enough resources to build anything.

The opponent has a city at 28, which is producing wheat. I need to reduce their resources to prevent them from gaining VP. The best option is to play a knight card, move the robber to 28, and steal resources. I have 4 brick and 4 ore, which are sufficient to play a knight card.

The opponent is at 2 VP and is close to victory. I have 4 VP and a 4-length longest road. I need to secure my longest road and get more VP. Building a road to expand my longest road is the best option. The BRICK port is available, but I don't have BRICKs in my hand. The WHEAT port is available, and I have 4 WHEATs, so I will trade 4 WHEATs for 1 BRICK.
Need to build to increase VP. Can't build a settlement or city now due to costs. Trading is the best option to gain resources and potentially build later.
No development cards to play, no immediate need for resources or development. End turn to pass the opportunity to the opponent.

The opponent has a longer road and more VP. I need to increase my resource collection to catch up. Trading 4 wheat for wood is the best option as I have 4 wheat in hand and can use wood to build roads or settlements. The 4:1 rate is favorable.

I need to build a road to expand my network and potentially get access to new resources. The road between (24, 53) connects to my existing road and gives access to a brick port, which is useful for future development card purchases. No other road options provide as much immediate strategic value.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have excess brick resources. Discarding bricks will help me stay under the 7-card limit and avoid discarding other resources. I need to manage my resources carefully as I'm close to victory points and the opponent is building.
Need to reduce hand size due to 7 roll. Brick is most abundant in hand and discarding it will not impact future moves significantly.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the opponent has no brick cards. Discarding a brick would force the opponent to take a brick, which is not beneficial. The opponent has no resources, so trading or building is not advantageous. Discarding wheat or ore is also not beneficial as the opponent has none. Discarding a sheep is the best option as it is a common resource and will force the opponent to take a brick, which they need.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have 3 brick cards. Discarding a brick will force the opponent to steal from me, which is not beneficial. Sheep and ore are not currently producing, so discarding them is not useful. Wheat is produced on a tile (node 28) that I control, so discarding a wheat card will ensure I receive one wheat card from the bank.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have excess brick cards. Discarding a brick card will force the opponent to take a resource from the bank, which is beneficial. No other actions are viable given the current resource surplus and the opponent's limited resources.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile (5, 0, 0) and moving it would disrupt production. The opponent has a settlement on a BRICK-producing tile (52, 0, 0), so moving the robber to this tile would allow stealing from the opponent. Additionally, the opponent has a longer road, so preventing them from extending it is beneficial.
No development cards available to buy. Need to end turn as no other actions can be taken.

End turn since there are no immediate actions that benefit me more than ending the turn. The opponent has a city and more roads, and I don't have any development cards to play. The robber is on a brick-producing tile, but there's no immediate way to benefit from it.

I need to build a road or settlement to gain more resources and VP. The opponent has 2 VP and I have 4, so I need to increase my resource collection to get closer to 10 VP. Building a road to connect to my city at node 28 would allow me to claim a new sheep-producing tile, which is valuable. I also have 4 ore, which I can trade for other resources.

The robber is on a brick tile, so no one gets brick resources. Red has a city at 28 producing wheat and has a strong road network. Building a settlement at node 32 (SHEEP on 4 (3 pips)) would be valuable, but it's not possible since it's not two edges away from any existing settlement. Building a road to connect to the existing road network or to a port is more strategic. Building a road from node 29 to node 30 is a good move since it connects to a port and gives access to brick resources.

The opponent is close to 10 VP and has a strong road network. I need to secure more VP quickly. I have no development cards and limited pieces left. Building a road is the only immediate action, but I need to consider future moves. Building a road from node 2 to node 3 does not help much. Building a road from node 4 to node 5 is redundant. Building a road from node 5 to node 15 is also not beneficial. Building a road from node 28 to node 29 is redundant. Building a road from node 29 to node 30 is also not beneficial. I should use my development card to place two roads for free, which is the best use of my current resources.
Need to increase VP. Have a city and a long road. Can't build a settlement or city now. Playing a knight card is the best option to gain VP.

The robber is currently on the BRICK tile, which produces on 5, 6, and 10. Moving the robber to a tile that produces more resources for me would be beneficial. The WHEAT tile produces on 3, 5, 8, and 11, and the ORE tile produces on 4, 8, and 11. Moving the robber to either of these tiles would stop them from producing and give me a resource advantage. Moving to the WHEAT tile is more advantageous as it produces on 3 and 5, which are more likely rolls.

The robber is currently on a BRICK producing tile (5, 0, 0), which produces BRICKs on a roll of 5 or 6. Moving the robber to a tile that doesn't produce resources is crucial. The opponent has a settlement on a BRICK producing tile (52, -1, 1), so moving the robber there would steal a BRICK from the opponent. This is a good move to gain an advantage.
The opponent has a 2 VP lead and only 2 VP. Buying a development card now could help catch up. The opponent has no resources in hand, so a Year of Plenty would be effective.
Need to gain VP or resources. Playing a knight card can move the robber and steal resources, potentially gaining VP or resources. No other action offers immediate benefits.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile (5, -1, 1), which produces BRICKs on 5 and 6. Moving the robber to a tile that does not produce resources will prevent BRICK production and also prevent the opponent from stealing. The best option is to move the robber to a tile that does not produce any resources, such as (0, 0, 0).

The robber needs to be moved to block the opponent's city at 28, which produces wheat and ore. Moving the robber to (0, -2, 2) will block this tile, preventing the opponent from producing and stealing their resources.

The opponent has a city and 2 VP, while I have a city and 6 VP. I need to secure more VP to win. I have 2 cities left to build, but the opponent has a longer road and can block my longest road. Trading is the best option to gain resources. Trading ore for wood is the most beneficial as I have a wood port and the opponent does not have a wood port.

The opponent has a 2 VP lead and only 2 VP. Trading is not beneficial as I have no resources to trade and the opponent has limited resources. Building is the best option to gain more settlements and cities. Building a settlement at node 9 would be the best as it is connected to a road and is not too close to the opponent's settlement.
Need resources to build. Can't afford a settlement or city. Ports offer better trades. Wood is most needed. Best trade is BRICK for WOOD.

The opponent is behind in VP and resources, but has a longer road. The best option is to trade wheat for brick to build a road. This can help expand and potentially block the opponent's road expansion.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the opponent has 3 brick cards. Trading 4 ore for 1 brick is not beneficial as I don't have any brick cards. The opponent has 3 sheep cards and 3 wheat cards, but trading ore for wheat or wood is not optimal as I already have enough wheat and wood. Trading ore for sheep is not beneficial as I don't have any sheep cards. The best option is to end the turn as I have no immediate benefit from trading.

I need to discard resources to comply with the 7-card limit. The opponent has more VP and a longer road, so I should prioritize discarding resources to avoid a 7 and maintain my resources.

Must discard as 7 was rolled and hand has 1 wheat. Ore is more valuable but cannot discard now.

The opponent has a 2 VP lead and is on track to win. I need to increase my VP and resource collection. Building a city at 28 will increase my VP and resource collection, but I need ore, which I don't have. Trading for ore is the best option to prepare for future city building.

Need to reduce hand size due to 7 being rolled. Sheep are more valuable, but discarding wheat is necessary to comply with the rule.

Discard an ORE as I have more than 7 resources and need to reduce my hand size. The opponent has more wheat and I can use this to my advantage later.
